headline: US Supreme Court gives Trump the power to fire top officials across the administration – except at the Fed
description: With a conservative majority, the highest court in the United States handed President Trump the authority on June 29 to dismiss leaders of independent federal agencies at will, with the sole exception of the central bank.

headline: The killing of West Bank children by Israel's army, a product of Palestinians' dehumanization
France passes watered-down legislation to curb fast fashion
2026 World Cup: Germany eliminated by Paraguay in stunning upset
description: Paraguay beat Germany in a penalty shootout, a shocking result early in the World Cup Round of 32. The Germans missed three penalty kicks to lose the shootout 4-3 after a 1-1 draw in Foxborough, Massachusetts. Paraguay now faces the daunting prospect of running into an in-form France next should Les Bleus beat Sweden.

headline: 'We should not accept that heatwaves cause thousands of deaths. That is not normal'
description: In an interview with Le Monde, Julien Dossier, founder of the environmental consulting firm Quattrolibri and the author of 'Renaissance écologique,' offers an initial political assessment of how France responded to the most intense heatwave ever recorded in the country.

headline: Macron brings back viral aviator sunglasses
description: At a ceremony with the Sultan of Oman, President Emmanuel Macron once again sported his now-iconic aviator sunglasses, which he debuted at the Davos World Economic Forum in January, in a speech featuring the English phrase 'for sure.'

headline: 'The heatwave shows, beyond any doubt, that without strong mitigation measures, adaptation will be impossible'
description: Efforts to combat climate change require far more than mere technical adjustments, writes Le Monde's Stéphane Foucart in his column.

'Life, Larry and the Pursuit of Unhappiness' on HBO Max: An irreverent take on key moments in US history
description: Produced by Michelle and Barack Obama, the limited series lets Larry David, co-creator of 'Seinfeld,' take a comedic wrecking ball to American mythology.

headline: From France to Italy, inside the counterfeit rings circulating fake banknotes across Europe via social media
description: A joint French-Italian investigation that lasted over a year led to the indictment of 12 people suspected of belonging to a huge counterfeiting network.

headline: French research thrown into turmoil by a multifaceted crisis
description: France's scientific community faces a host of challenges, from dwindling financial resources, ideological attacks and the emergence of artificial intelligence. The result is worrying for researchers nationwide, at a time when France is also slipping behind in scientific publishing.

headline: Kais Saied versus Tunisia's civil society

'The threat of force was a far more effective deterrent than Trump's show of force in Iran'
